description_long: This is a rectangular, black stereo sound system with a robust, boxy build and a visible power cord extending from the lower section. The front face features a perforated speaker grille covering most of the surface, above which is a metallic control panel with several round knobs, a switch, and a prominent red button. The top of the unit has a recessed handle for easy carrying, and the sides are plain with subtle corner reinforcements. The back panel is solid and unadorned except for the cord exit, suggesting internal speaker components. The overall design is utilitarian, suitable for use in garages, parks, or urban street performances, and the materials appear to be a mix of metal, plastic, and possibly wood for acoustic resonance.
description: A black, box-shaped stereo sound system with a perforated front grille, metallic control panel, and a visible power cord. It features multiple knobs, a red button, and a top handle for portability.
description_view_0: Front view showing the perforated speaker grille, metallic control panel with knobs and a red button, and the power cord extending outward.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the plain, solid side panel and the power cord trailing along the ground.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing a solid back panel with the power cord emerging from the lower section, no visible controls or grilles.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the plain side and the power cord, with a slight glimpse of the control panel edge.
attribute_speaker_grille_pattern: Dense grid of small, evenly spaced circular perforations covering the front speaker area.
attribute_control_panel_layout: A horizontal metallic strip at the top front with five round knobs, a toggle switch, and a large red rectangular button, all spaced evenly.
